payload.description:
# Story: Host applications can require registered Python modules from Tactus Lua

## Intent

As a Tactus host application, I want to register a Python-backed module on a runtime so that sandboxed Lua code can import host functionality with `require("module_name")` without adding host-specific code to the Tactus standard library.

## Gherkin

```gherkin
Feature: Host-registered Python modules are importable from Tactus Lua

  Scenario: Registered module can be required by name
    Given a TactusRuntime has registered a Python module named "plexus"
    When sandboxed Lua executes `local plexus = require("plexus")`
    Then the returned value is the Python module object exposed through the sandbox bridge
    And Lua can call a method on that module

  Scenario: Unregistered module remains unavailable
    Given no host module named "not_registered" has been registered
    When sandboxed Lua executes `require("not_registered")`
    Then Tactus raises a module-not-found error

  Scenario: Tactus stdlib imports continue to work
    Given the Tactus standard library loader supports existing `tactus.*` Python modules
    When sandboxed Lua requires a supported `tactus.*` module
    Then the behavior is unchanged from before host-module support
```

## Definition of Done

- Public host API exists to register a module name and object.
- Host-registered modules are available via the existing safe `require` path.
- Existing stdlib module loading behavior is preserved.
- Tests cover registered module import, unknown module rejection, and stdlib compatibility.
payload.title: Host applications can require registered Python modules from Tactus Lua
